zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Blyant於1379年出生在英國英格蘭素福爾可斯托克 白 易普斯維其時，她的父親Simon Blyant27歲，母親Agnes de Bresworth22歲。她於1405年與Richard Poley在英國英格蘭素福爾可斯托克 白 易普斯維其結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1443年在英國素福爾可易普斯維其享年64歲，埋葬在英國素福爾可巴德利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Blyant于1379年出生在英国英格兰素福爾可斯托克 白 易普斯維其时，她的父亲Simon Blyant27岁，母亲Agnes de Bresworth22岁。她于1405年与Richard Poley在英国英格兰素福爾可斯托克 白 易普斯維其结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1443年在英国素福爾可易普斯維其享年64岁，埋葬在英国素福爾可巴德利。

nameMeanings.name: Blyant
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Celtic personal name Brian (from brigo- ‘high’ + the suffix -ant-), with excrescent -t. Breton bearers of this name were among the Normans who invaded England in 1066. They went on to settle in Ireland in the 12th century, where the name mingled with the native Irish form Briain (see O'Brien ). The latter had also been borrowed, as Brján, by the Vikings, who introduced it independently into northwestern England before the Norman Conquest.
Breton: very rare variant of   Briant   (see   Briand  ) and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.
History: The American poet William Cullen Bryant (1794–1878) came of a New England family, being descended from Stephen Bryant, who had settled in Plymouth Colony in 1632.

variants.name: Brian
meanings: Irish and English: variant of   Bryant  .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Ó Briain, see O'Brien .
French: unflattering nickname from Old Occitan brian ‘mite, maggot’, by extension brimborion, possibly denoting a very small man.

variants.name: Bryan
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Bryant  .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Ó Briain, see O'Brien .

variants.name: Briant
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Andre, Laure.

variants.name: O'Brien
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Dermot, Liam, Brigid, Declan, Donovan, John Patrick, Kieran, Colm, Donal, Brennan, Caitlin.

Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Briain ‘descendant of Brian’, a personal name probably based on the element bre- ‘hill’, with the transferred sense ‘eminence’, i.e. ‘exalted one’. See also Bryan . In Ireland there has also been some confusion with O'Byrne (see Byrne ).
History: This name is borne in particular by one of the greatest of Irish septs, descendants of Brian Boru, who rose to the high kingship of Ireland in 1002.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Blyant was born in 1379, in Stoke by Ipswich, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, her father, Simon Blyant, was 27 and her mother, Agnes de Bresworth, was 22. She married Richard Poley in 1405, in Stoke by Ipswich, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. She died in 1443, in Ipswich, Suffolk, England, at the age of 64, and was buried in Badley, Suffolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Blyant est née en 1379, à Stoke by Ipswich, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Simon Blyant, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Agnes de Bresworth, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Richard Poley en 1405, à Stoke by Ipswich, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1443, à Ipswich, Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterrée à Badley, Suffolk, Angleterre.

Sie hat 1405 in Stoke by Ipswich, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom Richard Poley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb 1443 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Ipswich, Suffolk, England und wurde in Badley, Suffolk, England begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Blyantが1379年にイギリスイギリススフォルクストク ビー イプスウィチで生まれた時，父Simon Blyantは27歳，母Agnes de Bresworthは22歳であった。1405年にイギリスイギリススフォルクストク ビー イプスウィチでRichard Poleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1443年にイギリススフォルクイプスウィチで64歳で死去し，イギリススフォルクバドリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Blyant(이)가 1379년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 스토크 비 이프스위치에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Simon Blyant(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes de Bresworth(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1405년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 스토크 비 이프스위치에서 Richard Poley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1443년 영국 수폴크 이프스위치에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수폴크 바들리에 묻혔다.

ru.Stokes, Suffolk, , England: сток би ипсвич, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Blyant родилась в 1379 г. в местности сток би ипсвич, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Simon Blyant было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes de Bresworth, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Poley в 1405 г. в местности сток би ипсвич, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла в 1443 г. в местности ипсвич, суффолк, Англия в возрасте 64 лет и была похоронена в местности бадли, суффолк, Англия.

Se casó con Richard Poley en 1405, en Stoke by Ipswich, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en 1443, en Ipswich, Suffolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 64 años, y fue sepultada en Badley, Suffolk, Inglaterra.
